rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are provided
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Align helper declaration types with the expression types to avoid type mismatches

Remove generated clauses that involve range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in assumptions and guarantees
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Avoid placing assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
